← SUPERSLOTCAR.COM

PIT NOTES

Updates from the workshop, straight off the bench.

2026-07-15 THE BOARD GAME ON THE WORKBENCH People ask what this game actually is — a racing game with cards? A deckbuilder with wheels? Here's the honest origin: it exists because of Heat: Pedal to the Metal, the… 2026-07-15 ELEVEN CARDS NOW WEAR THE MOLTEN GOLD We sat down with all 128 cards and a pot of coffee and audited the lot — every stat, every price, every card that quietly won more runs than it had any right to. The… 2026-07-15 THE TRACK REMEMBERS EVERY CRASH Crashes used to be fireworks: a bang, a tumble, and thirty seconds later the circuit looked like nothing had ever happened. That amnesia is cured. A car that goes off… 2026-07-15 THE FIELD DRAFTS FOR REAL NOW Confession time: until recently, the rivals were stat lines with faces. Convincing faces! But while you were agonising over a mid-corner draft, they were just...… 2026-07-15 LIGHTS OUT, AND AWAY WE GO Races used to begin the way spreadsheets begin: everything just started existing. Now there's a proper ceremony. A light gantry drops in over the start line, the camera… 2026-07-15 THE MECHANIC TAKES WALK-INS There's a new stop on the map, between the shop's price tags and the mystery stop's dice: the Mechanic. She takes one look at your deck, wipes her hands on a rag, and… 2026-07-15 BACKTRACK: DRAW A LOOP, PRESS GO Some things shipped while the diary wasn't looking. This series catches up. First offender: the Track Builder, sitting right there on the main menu. 2026-07-15 SOME ENGINES SHOULD STAY UNDER THE TARP. Mystery stops had a habit of taking your money and handing back a shrug — pay the toll, watch the purse shrink, drive on feeling like you picked the wrong road. That's… 2026-07-14 CALM HANDS. RISING PRESSURE. CARDS EVERYWHERE. Our draft clock used to tighten by counting laps and, later, by repeatedly multiplying itself. That made a short sprint wait too long for the drama, an endurance race… 2026-07-13 THE SPONSOR SIGNS. THE WORLD OPENS. The signing gift used to finish with a polite stamp and then leave a stack of instructions taped across the top of the country. We have promoted the chosen hardware to a… 2026-07-13 MOON FROG, THEN MAYHEM The cabinet used to wake up halfway through its own lap. The track arrived first, one car took the scenic route around a corner, and a cold scenery build could turn that… 2026-07-13 FREEZE THE RACE. MOVE THE CAMERA. The miniature controls were technically live, but the race was not especially interested in holding still while you used them. Cars carried on with their afternoon… 2026-07-10 THE COUNTRY GETS THERE BEFORE YOU DO The road map had developed an unfortunate habit of keeping the checker curtain shut while the whole country found its trousers. Pressing START should feel like pressing… 2026-07-10 EVERY CLICK GETS A CLACK The cars already knew how to make a scene. The quieter bits around them did not. Some buttons landed like damp cardboard, a clean overtake could happen without so much… 2026-07-10 THE CONTROLLER GETS A PIT PASS We were pretending a controller could get around the garage. It could, technically. In practice, the Pit Shop was a wall of lovely things with no clear answer to the… 2026-07-09 THE JOB IS ON THE BOARD Before the lights, a normal race used to shout a vague little welcome in the same oversized type as GO. It had plenty of volume and not much information. 2026-07-09 THE TILL STOPS HIDING The Pit Shop had the money up in its letterhead. Technically visible, sure. But when you were looking from a rare card to a suspiciously expensive bit of hardware, the… 2026-07-09 FOUR BUTTONS, TWO OF THEM LIES Click a race on the map and a card slides up to introduce it: the crew you'd be lining up against, the one thing you have to do to walk away clean, what's in it for you… 2026-07-08 THE PIT BOARD LEARNS TO POINT Shove a rival hard enough and the paddock notices. The pit board slides up along the bottom of the screen with the offended party's face on it and a line about how their… 2026-07-08 THE ROAD SO FAR, PT. 1 — THE JANUARY TOY This is the first of a short series about how this game got here. Everything below is read straight out of the project's own history — real commits, real dates — and the… 2026-07-08 THE SIGNING DESK GETS ITS LETTERHEAD Every run opens the same way: your new sponsor slides three parts across the desk and you keep one. It's the first decision of the season — and until this week it looked… 2026-07-07 THE GARAGE GROWS: TEN BODY TYPES Until now every car on the grid wore the same body — the CLASSIC, the pointy little sports car the game shipped with. It's not going anywhere. But it's getting company…
